## Pattern D: SKOS Taxonomy (FHIR-Compatible)

### Discriminator

Restriction target is a class that:
- Is `rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept`, OR
- Has `owl:equivalentClass` with restriction on `skos:inScheme`

**ASG detection (SPARQL):**
```sparql
# Check for SKOS subclass
{ ?target rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept }
UNION
# Check for equivalentClass with inScheme restriction
{ ?target owl:equivalentClass ?equiv .
  ?equiv owl:onProperty skos:inScheme ;
         owl:hasValue ?scheme . }
```

### FHIR Terminology Concepts

| FHIR Resource | Purpose | OWL/SKOS Mapping |
|---------------|---------|------------------|
| **CodeSystem** | Defines codes and their meanings | `skos:ConceptScheme` |
| **ValueSet** | Selects codes from CodeSystem(s) for use in a context | `skos:Collection` with `skos:member` |
| **Coding** | Single code instance: system + code + display | `skos:Concept` with `skos:notation`, `skos:prefLabel` |
| **CodeableConcept** | Multiple Codings representing same concept | Array of Coding objects |
| **Binding** | Links element to ValueSet with strength | OWL restriction + `fhir:binding` annotation |

### Full FHIR-Compatible Model

#### 1. CodeSystem (defines the codes)

```turtle
sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem a skos:ConceptScheme ;
    rdfs:label "Measurement Context Code System"@en ;
    skos:definition "Code system defining measurement context types"@en ;
    fhir:url "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/CodeSystem/measurement-context"^^xsd:anyURI ;
    fhir:version "1.0.0" ;
    fhir:status "active" ;
    skos:hasTopConcept sensor:context_continuous,
                       sensor:context_discrete .
```

#### 2. Concepts (defined by the CodeSystem)

```turtle
sensor:context_continuous a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:topConceptOf sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "continuous" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Continuous Measurement"@en ;
    skos:definition "Ongoing continuous measurement"@en ;
    skos:narrower sensor:context_realtime,
                  sensor:context_sampled .

sensor:context_realtime a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "realtime" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Real-time"@en ;
    skos:altLabel "Live"@en ;
    skos:definition "Real-time continuous streaming"@en ;
    skos:broader sensor:context_continuous ;
    skos:narrower sensor:context_realtime_high_freq,
                  sensor:context_realtime_standard .

sensor:context_realtime_high_freq a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "realtime-hf" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Real-time High Frequency"@en ;
    skos:definition "High-frequency real-time (>1000 samples/sec)"@en ;
    skos:broader sensor:context_realtime .

sensor:context_realtime_standard a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "realtime-std" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Real-time Standard"@en ;
    skos:definition "Standard frequency real-time (1-1000 samples/sec)"@en ;
    skos:broader sensor:context_realtime .

sensor:context_discrete a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:topConceptOf sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "discrete" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Discrete Measurement"@en ;
    skos:definition "Point-in-time discrete measurements"@en ;
    skos:narrower sensor:context_scheduled,
                  sensor:context_triggered .

sensor:context_scheduled a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "scheduled" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Scheduled"@en ;
    skos:altLabel "Batch"@en ;
    skos:definition "Measurement at scheduled times"@en ;
    skos:broader sensor:context_discrete .

sensor:context_triggered a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "triggered" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Event Triggered"@en ;
    skos:altLabel "On-Demand"@en ;
    skos:definition "Measurement triggered by external event"@en ;
    skos:broader sensor:context_discrete ;
    skos:narrower sensor:context_triggered_alarm,
                  sensor:context_triggered_threshold .
```

#### 3. ValueSet (selects codes for a specific use)

**Full CodeSystem inclusion:**
```turtle
sensor:AllMeasurementContextsValueSet a skos:Collection ;
    rdfs:label "All Measurement Contexts Value Set"@en ;
    skos:definition "Value set including all measurement context codes"@en ;
    fhir:url "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/all-measurement-contexts"^^xsd:anyURI ;
    fhir:version "1.0.0" ;
    fhir:status "active" .
    # Membership defined via bridge class equivalentClass
```

**Subset ValueSet:**
```turtle
sensor:RealtimeMeasurementValueSet a skos:Collection ;
    rdfs:label "Realtime Measurement Value Set"@en ;
    skos:definition "Value set for real-time measurement contexts only"@en ;
    fhir:url "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/realtime-measurement"^^xsd:anyURI ;
    fhir:version "1.0.0" ;
    fhir:status "active" ;
    skos:member sensor:context_realtime,
                sensor:context_realtime_high_freq,
                sensor:context_realtime_standard .
```

#### 4. Bridge Class (makes ValueSet usable in OWL restrictions)

**Bridge for all contexts (full CodeSystem):**
```turtle
sensor:MeasurementContextCode a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:label "Measurement Context Code"@en ;
    rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept ;
    owl:equivalentClass [
        a owl:Restriction ;
        owl:onProperty skos:inScheme ;
        owl:hasValue sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem
    ] ;
    fhir:binding [
        fhir:valueSet "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/all-measurement-contexts"^^xsd:anyURI ;
        fhir:strength "required"
    ] .
```

**Bridge for subset (Realtime only):**
```turtle
sensor:RealtimeMeasurementCode a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:label "Realtime Measurement Code"@en ;
    rdfs:subClassOf sensor:MeasurementContextCode ;
    owl:equivalentClass [
        a owl:Class ;
        owl:intersectionOf (
            skos:Concept
            [ a owl:Restriction ;
              owl:onProperty skos:inScheme ;
              owl:hasValue sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ]
            [ a owl:Restriction ;
              owl:onProperty skos:broader ;
              owl:someValuesFrom [ owl:oneOf ( sensor:context_realtime ) ] ]
        )
    ] ;
    fhir:binding [
        fhir:valueSet "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/realtime-measurement"^^xsd:anyURI ;
        fhir:strength "required"
    ] .
```

#### 5. Binding (restriction on domain class)

```turtle
sensor:Sensor rdfs:subClassOf [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
    owl:someValuesFrom sensor:MeasurementContextCode  # All contexts allowed
] .

sensor:RealtimeSensor rdfs:subClassOf
    sensor:Sensor ,
    [ a owl:Restriction ;
      owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
      owl:someValuesFrom sensor:RealtimeMeasurementCode  # Only realtime subset
    ] .
```

### All Variations

```turtle
sensor:PatternD rdf:type owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf
        # D1: Required array (1..*) via someValuesFrom
        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
          owl:someValuesFrom sensor:MeasurementContextCode
        ] ,
        # D2: Type constraint (0..*) via allValuesFrom
        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
          owl:allValuesFrom sensor:MeasurementContextCode
        ] ,
        # D3: Required single (1..1) via qualifiedCardinality
        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
          owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
          owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
        ] ,
        # D4: Optional single (0..1) via maxQualifiedCardinality
        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
          owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
          owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
        ] ,
        # D5: Bounded array (1..3) via min + max
        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
          owl:minQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
          owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
        ] ,
        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
          owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "3"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
          owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
        ] ;
    skos:definition "SKOS Taxonomy. Best for: Standards-compliant vocabularies" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Pattern D" .
```

```yaml
PatternD:
  type: object
  properties:
    hasMeasurementContext:
      type: array
      items:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
```

### Variation D3: Required Single (1..1) via qualifiedCardinality

**OWL:**
```turtle
owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
PatternD:
  type: object
  required:
    - hasMeasurementContext
  properties:
    hasMeasurementContext:
      $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
```

### Variation D4: Optional Single (0..1) via maxQualifiedCardinality

**OWL:**
```turtle
owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
```

**OpenAPI:**

```yaml
  type: object
  properties:
    hasMeasurementContext:
      $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
```

### Variation D5: Bounded Array (1..3) via min + max

**OWL:**
```turtle
owl:minQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
# Combined with:
owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "3"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
```

**OpenAPI:**

```yaml
  type: object

    - hasMeasurementContext
  properties:
    hasMeasurementContext:
      type: array
      minItems: 1

        $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
```

### OpenAPI Schemas for FHIR-Compatible Output

**Coding (single code):**
```yaml
components:
  schemas:
    MeasurementContextCoding:
      type: object
      required:
        - code
      properties:
        system:
          type: string
          const: "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/CodeSystem/measurement-context"
          description: The CodeSystem URL
        code:
          type: string
          description: The code value (skos:notation)
        display:
          type: string
          description: Human-readable display (skos:prefLabel)
      x-fhir-valueset: "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/all-measurement-contexts"
      x-fhir-binding-strength: required
      x-skos-hierarchy:
        continuous:
          children: [realtime, sampled]
        realtime:
          parent: continuous
          children: [realtime-hf, realtime-std]
        realtime-hf:
          parent: realtime
        realtime-std:
          parent: realtime
        sampled:
          parent: continuous
        discrete:
          children: [scheduled, triggered]
        scheduled:
          parent: discrete
        triggered:
          parent: discrete
          children: [triggered-alarm, triggered-threshold]
```

**CodeableConcept (multiple codings):**
```yaml
    MeasurementContextCodeableConcept:
      type: object
      properties:
        coding:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
          description: Code(s) from controlled vocabularies
        text:
          type: string
          description: Plain text representation chosen by user
```

### Pattern D Discovery (ASG)

```sparql
# Find all concepts in a scheme
SELECT ?concept ?code ?display ?broader WHERE {
    ?concept skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
             skos:notation ?code ;
             skos:prefLabel ?display .
    OPTIONAL { ?concept skos:broader ?broader }
}
ORDER BY ?broader ?code
```

### FHIR Binding Strengths

| Strength | Meaning | OWL Pattern |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| **required** | Must be from ValueSet | `owl:someValuesFrom` + `owl:allValuesFrom` |
| **extensible** | Should be from ValueSet, can extend | `owl:someValuesFrom` only |
| **preferred** | Encouraged but not required | `owl:someValuesFrom` with broader class |
| **example** | Illustrative only | `rdfs:range` only (no restriction) |

### Pattern C vs D Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern C (Class-based) | Pattern D (SKOS) |
|--------|------------------------|------------------|
| Standard | OWL only | OWL + SKOS + FHIR |
| Hierarchy | `rdfs:subClassOf` | `skos:broader`/`skos:narrower` |
| Membership | `rdf:type` | `skos:inScheme` |
| Subset selection | Subclass hierarchy | `skos:Collection` + `skos:member` |
| Multi-language | Not native | `skos:prefLabel`/`skos:altLabel` with `@lang` |
| External mapping | Not native | `skos:exactMatch`, `skos:closeMatch` |
| Interoperability | OWL tools only | FHIR, SKOS, terminology servers |
| Discovery | `?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* Class` | `?x skos:inScheme Scheme` |

### Pattern D Advantages

| Capability | Pattern A/B/C | Pattern D |
|------------|---------------|-----------|
| FHIR compatibility | ✗ | ✓ |
| Terminology server support | ✗ | ✓ |
| Multi-language labels | ✗ | ✓ |
| Alternative labels | ✗ | ✓ (`skos:altLabel`) |
| External code mapping | ✗ | ✓ (`skos:exactMatch`) |
| Transitive hierarchy queries | Limited | ✓ (`skos:broader+`) |
| ValueSet subsetting | ✗ | ✓ |
| Binding strength | ✗ | ✓ |

### Comparison: Pattern D vs FHIR RDF Ontology

The official FHIR RDF specification takes a different approach to terminology than our SKOS-based Pattern D. Here's how they compare:

#### FHIR RDF Ontology Approach

FHIR RDF represents coded values as **structured objects** with properties, not as direct concept references:

```turtle
# FHIR RDF Coding structure
fhir:CodingBase.system rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .
fhir:CodingBase.code rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .
fhir:CodingBase.display rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .
fhir:CodingBase.version rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .

# Example instance (FHIR RDF style)
:observation1 fhir:Observation.status [
    fhir:Coding.system [ fhir:value "http://hl7.org/fhir/observation-status"^^xsd:anyURI ] ;
    fhir:Coding.code [ fhir:value "final" ] ;
    fhir:Coding.display [ fhir:value "Final" ]
] .
```

#### Pattern D (SKOS) Approach

Pattern D uses **direct concept references** with SKOS vocabulary:

```turtle
# Pattern D structure
sensor:context_realtime a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "realtime" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Real-time"@en .

# Example instance (Pattern D style)
:sensor1 sensor:hasMeasurementContext sensor:context_realtime .
```

#### Key Differences

| Aspect | FHIR RDF Ontology | Pattern D (SKOS) |
|--------|-------------------|------------------|
| **Code representation** | Anonymous node with properties | Named individual (IRI) |
| **Value access** | `fhir:Coding.code/fhir:value` | Direct object property |
| **System identification** | Explicit `fhir:Coding.system` property | Implicit via `skos:inScheme` |
| **Display text** | Explicit `fhir:Coding.display` property | `skos:prefLabel` on concept |
| **Hierarchy** | Not in Coding structure | `skos:broader/narrower` on concepts |
| **Round-tripping** | Optimized for JSON/XML ↔ RDF | Native RDF/OWL semantics |
| **Reasoning** | Limited (data-oriented) | Full OWL DL reasoning |
| **External ontologies** | Optional `rdf:type` extension | `skos:exactMatch`, `skos:closeMatch` |

#### FHIR RDF Extensions for Ontology Integration

FHIR RDF provides optional extensions to bridge to external ontologies:

```json
{
  "code": {
    "extension": {
      "url": "http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/rdftype",
      "valueUri": "http://snomed.info/id/24484000"
    },
    "coding": {
      "system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
      "code": "24484000",
      "display": "Severe"
    }
  }
}
```

This allows FHIR instances to carry an `rdf:type` assertion linking to external ontology concepts, while maintaining JSON/XML compatibility.

#### When to Use Each Approach

| Use Case | Recommended Approach |
|----------|---------------------|
| Exchanging FHIR resources | FHIR RDF (for round-tripping) |
| Internal ontology modeling | Pattern D (SKOS) |
| Terminology server integration | Either (both support FHIR API) |
| OWL reasoning over codes | Pattern D (SKOS) |
| Multi-system code mapping | Pattern D (via `skos:exactMatch`) |
| Generating APIs from ontology | Pattern D (cleaner mapping) |

#### Hybrid Approach for FHIR Interoperability

For maximum interoperability, you can support both:

```turtle
# Define concepts in SKOS (Pattern D)
sensor:context_realtime a skos:Concept ;
    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
    skos:notation "realtime" ;
    skos:prefLabel "Real-time"@en ;
    # Map to FHIR coding
    fhir:system "https://graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/CodeSystem/measurement-context"^^xsd:anyURI ;
    fhir:code "realtime" ;
    fhir:display "Real-time" .

# In instance data, use direct reference (Pattern D style)
:sensor1 sensor:hasMeasurementContext sensor:context_realtime .

# For FHIR export, generate Coding structure
# {
#   "system": "https://graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/CodeSystem/measurement-context",
#   "code": "realtime",
#   "display": "Real-time"
# }
```

This allows:
1. **Native OWL reasoning** via direct concept references
2. **FHIR JSON/XML export** via properties on concepts
3. **Terminology server queries** via SKOS structure

---

## ASG Decision Tree

```
1. EXTRACT RESTRICTION from class definition
   Check both rdfs:subClassOf and owl:equivalentClass

2. EXTRACT TARGET from restriction
   ├─ someValuesFrom → target
   ├─ allValuesFrom → target
   └─ onClass → target

3. IS TARGET A BLANK NODE?
   └─ YES → Check for owl:oneOf inside blank node
            └─ Found owl:oneOf? Extract individuals from list
               └─ Do individuals have a domain class type (not just owl:NamedIndividual)?
                  ├─ YES → Pattern B (typed individuals)
                  │        Parse oneOf, emit enum with x-code-metadata
                  └─ NO  → Pattern A (inline enum)
                           Parse oneOf, emit simple inline enum
   └─ NO (target is named class IRI) → Continue to step 4

4. DOES TARGET HAVE apiLabel MATCHING CURRENT API SUFFIX?
   └─ NO  → Stop traversal (not included in this API)
   └─ YES → Continue to step 5

5. IS TARGET rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept?
   OR HAS owl:equivalentClass WITH skos:inScheme RESTRICTION?
   └─ YES → Pattern D (SKOS)
            Query: ?x skos:inScheme <scheme>
            Emit: Coding schema with hierarchy
   └─ NO  → Continue to step 6

6. DOES TARGET HAVE owl:oneOf (direct or via equivalentClass)?
   └─ YES → Pattern B variant (named class enum)
            Parse oneOf list, emit enum schema with x-code-metadata
   └─ NO  → Continue to step 7

7. DOES TARGET HAVE OUTGOING OBJECT PROPERTY RESTRICTIONS?
   (i.e., rdfs:subClassOf [ owl:onProperty ?p ] where ?p is owl:ObjectProperty
    pointing to another domain class)
   └─ YES → Domain class, not taxonomy
            Continue property traversal into this class
   └─ NO  → Pattern C (class-based taxonomy)
            Query: ?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* <TargetClass>
            Emit: $ref to extensible schema with x-hierarchy
```

---

## Cardinality Summary

| OWL Pattern | Cardinality | Required? | Array? |
|-------------|-------------|-----------|--------|
| `owl:qualifiedCardinality 1` | 1..1 | Yes | No |
| `owl:maxQualifiedCardinality 1` | 0..1 | No | No |
| `owl:someValuesFrom` | 1..* | Yes | Yes |
| `owl:allValuesFrom` (alone) | 0..* | No | Yes |
| `owl:someValuesFrom` + `owl:allValuesFrom` | 1..* (constrained) | Yes | Yes |
| `owl:minQualifiedCardinality n` | n..* | Yes (if n≥1) | Yes |
| `owl:maxQualifiedCardinality n` | 0..n | No | Yes (bounded) |

---

## Annotation Requirements

| Pattern | What needs `apiLabel` matching API suffix |
|---------|------------------------------------------|
| A | Property only (inline `owl:oneOf` parsed, individuals have no class) |
| B | Property + individuals (individuals have `apiLabel` for API inclusion) |
| C | Property + taxonomy class + subclasses (if hierarchical) |
| D | Property + bridge class (concepts discovered via `skos:inScheme`) |

**Pattern A vs B annotation difference:**
- Pattern A: Individuals discovered from `owl:oneOf`, no `apiLabel` needed on individuals
- Pattern B: Individuals have domain class and `apiLabel` for multi-API support

**Pattern C annotation notes:**
- The taxonomy class (`EnvironmentCode`) needs `apiLabel`
- Subclasses (`IndoorEnvironment`, `IndustrialEnvironment`, `OutdoorEnvironment`) need `apiLabel` if they should appear in the API hierarchy
- Individuals are discovered via query, so they don't need `apiLabel` unless filtered by API

**Pattern D annotation notes:**
- The bridge class (`MeasurementContextCode`) needs `apiLabel`
- The `skos:ConceptScheme` does NOT need `apiLabel` (it's not an OWL class)
- `skos:Concept` instances do NOT need `apiLabel` — discovered via `skos:inScheme` query
- ValueSet collections do NOT need `apiLabel` — referenced via bridge class binding

1→
     2→# Introduction
     3→We are adding some new patterns for defining taxonomies, which may be more complex than just the string enums we currently support. 
     4→
     5→## Modifications
     6→- ResourceSchemaMapper should recognize these patterns and use a new TaxonomyPattenMapper to generate the schemas
     7→- recognition is using OWL classes and the SchemeVisitor (which may need extending). The sparql examples are just a guid
     8→- Modify ResourceSchemaMapper in keeping with its current structure
     9→- add unit and integration tests in keeping with the current patterns
    10→
    11→
    12→# GRL Generator: Taxonomy Pattern Reference
    13→
    14→This document provides a complete reference for taxonomy patterns supported by the GRL Generator (ASG), including discriminators for pattern detection, OWL variations, and expected OpenAPI outputs.
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Pattern Summary
    19→
    20→| Pattern | Description | Discriminator | OpenAPI Output |
    21→|---------|-------------|---------------|----------------|
    22→| A | Inline Enumeration (untyped) | Blank node with `owl:oneOf`; individuals have no domain class | Inline `enum` |
    23→| B | Typed Individuals | Blank node with `owl:oneOf`; individuals have domain class + metadata | `enum` with string values |
    24→| C | Class-based Taxonomy | Named class IRI target; no `owl:oneOf`; no object property restrictions; flat or hierarchical | `$ref` to extensible schema (with `x-hierarchy` if hierarchical) |
    25→| D | SKOS Taxonomy | Class is `subClassOf skos:Concept` or has `skos:inScheme` restriction | `$ref` to Coding schema |
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Pattern A: Inline Enumeration (No Type)
    30→
    31→### Discriminator
    32→
    33→The restriction target (`owl:onClass`, `owl:someValuesFrom`, or `owl:allValuesFrom`) is a **blank node** (anonymous class) containing `owl:oneOf`. The individuals referenced in the `owl:oneOf` list have no domain-specific class—they are just `owl:NamedIndividual`.
    34→
    35→The restriction can be attached via either `rdfs:subClassOf` or `owl:equivalentClass`.
    36→
    37→**ASG detection (SPARQL):**
    38→```sparql
    39→# Target is blank node with oneOf, via either subClassOf or equivalentClass
    40→{
    41→    ?class rdfs:subClassOf ?restriction .
    42→} UNION {
    43→    ?class owl:equivalentClass ?restriction .
    44→}
    45→?restriction owl:onProperty ?prop .
    46→?restriction (owl:someValuesFrom|owl:allValuesFrom|owl:onClass) ?target .
    47→FILTER(isBlank(?target))
    48→?target owl:oneOf ?list .
    49→```
    50→
    51→### Individuals Definition
    52→
    53→```turtle
    54→sensor:Active a owl:NamedIndividual ; rdfs:label "Active" .
    55→sensor:Cancelled a owl:NamedIndividual ; rdfs:label "Cancelled" .
    56→sensor:Hold a owl:NamedIndividual ; rdfs:label "Hold" .
    57→```
    58→
    59→### All Variations
    60→
    61→```turtle
    62→sensor:PatternA rdf:type owl:Class ;
    63→    rdfs:subClassOf
    64→        # A1: Required array (1..*) via someValuesFrom
    65→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
    66→          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
    67→          owl:someValuesFrom [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
    68→                               owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active
    69→                                           sensor:Cancelled )
    70→                             ]
    71→        ] ,
    72→        # A2: Type constraint (0..*) via allValuesFrom
    73→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
    74→          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
    75→          owl:allValuesFrom [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
    76→                              owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active
    77→                                          sensor:Cancelled )
    78→                            ]
    79→        ] ,
    80→        # A3: Required array (1..*) via minQualifiedCardinality
    81→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
    82→          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
    83→          owl:minQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
    84→          owl:onClass [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
    85→                        owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active
    86→                                    sensor:Cancelled )
    87→                      ]
    88→        ] ,
    89→        # A4: Required single (1..1) via qualifiedCardinality
    90→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
    91→          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
    92→          owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
    93→          owl:onClass [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
    94→                        owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active
    95→                                    sensor:Cancelled
    96→                                    sensor:Hold )
    97→                      ]
    98→        ] ,
    99→        # A5: Bounded array (0..3) via maxQualifiedCardinality
   100→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   101→          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
   102→          owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "3"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   103→          owl:onClass [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
   104→                        owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active
   105→                                    sensor:Cancelled )
   106→                      ]
   107→        ] ;
   108→    skos:prefLabel "Pattern A" .
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Variation A1: Required Array (1..*) via someValuesFrom
   112→
   113→**OWL:**
   114→```turtle
   115→owl:someValuesFrom [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
   116→                     owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active sensor:Cancelled ) ]
   117→```
   118→
   119→**OpenAPI:**
   120→```yaml
   121→PatternA:
   122→  type: object
   123→  required:
   124→    - isCategorisedBy
   125→  properties:
   126→    isCategorisedBy:
   127→      type: array
   128→      minItems: 1
   129→      items:
   130→        type: string
   131→        enum: [Active, Cancelled]
   132→```
   133→
   134→### Variation A2: Type Constraint / Optional Array (0..*) via allValuesFrom
   135→
   136→**OWL:**
   137→```turtle
   138→owl:allValuesFrom [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
   139→                    owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active sensor:Cancelled ) ]
   140→```
   141→
   142→**OpenAPI:**
   143→```yaml
   144→PatternA:
   145→  type: object
   146→  properties:
   147→    isCategorisedBy:
   148→      type: array
   149→      items:
   150→        type: string
   151→        enum: [Active, Cancelled]
   152→```
   153→
   154→### Variation A3: Required Array (1..*) via minQualifiedCardinality
   155→
   156→**OWL:**
   157→```turtle
   158→owl:minQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   159→owl:onClass [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
   160→              owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active sensor:Cancelled ) ]
   161→```
   162→
   163→**OpenAPI:**
   164→```yaml
   165→PatternA:
   166→  type: object
   167→  required:
   168→    - isCategorisedBy
   169→  properties:
   170→    isCategorisedBy:
   171→      type: array
   172→      minItems: 1
   173→      items:
   174→        type: string
   175→        enum: [Active, Cancelled]
   176→```
   177→
   178→### Variation A4: Required Single (1..1) via qualifiedCardinality
   179→
   180→**OWL:**
   181→```turtle
   182→owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   183→owl:onClass [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
   184→              owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active sensor:Cancelled sensor:Hold ) ]
   185→```
   186→
   187→**OpenAPI:**
   188→```yaml
   189→PatternA:
   190→  type: object
   191→  required:
   192→    - isCategorisedBy
   193→  properties:
   194→    isCategorisedBy:
   195→      type: string
   196→      enum: [Active, Cancelled, Hold]
   197→```
   198→
   199→### Variation A5: Bounded Array (0..3) via maxQualifiedCardinality
   200→
   201→**OWL:**
   202→```turtle
   203→owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "3"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   204→owl:onClass [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
   205→              owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active sensor:Cancelled ) ]
   206→```
   207→
   208→**OpenAPI:**
   209→```yaml
   210→PatternA:
   211→  type: object
   212→  properties:
   213→    isCategorisedBy:
   214→      type: array
   215→      maxItems: 3
   216→      items:
   217→        type: string
   218→        enum: [Active, Cancelled]
   219→```
   220→
   221→### Variation A6: Optional Single (0..1) via maxQualifiedCardinality 1
   222→
   223→**OWL:**
   224→```turtle
   225→owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   226→owl:onClass [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
   227→              owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active sensor:Cancelled ) ]
   228→```
   229→
   230→**OpenAPI:**
   231→```yaml
   232→PatternA:
   233→  type: object
   234→  properties:
   235→    isCategorisedBy:
   236→      type: string
   237→      enum: [Active, Cancelled]
   238→```
   239→
   240→### Variation A7: Via owl:equivalentClass (instead of rdfs:subClassOf)
   241→
   242→The restriction can be attached using `owl:equivalentClass` rather than `rdfs:subClassOf`. This changes the semantics (necessary AND sufficient vs necessary only) but the ASG traversal and output are identical.
   243→
   244→**OWL:**
   245→```turtle
   246→sensor:PatternA rdf:type owl:Class ;
   247→    owl:equivalentClass [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   248→                          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
   249→                          owl:someValuesFrom [ rdf:type owl:Class ;
   250→                                               owl:oneOf ( sensor:Active
   251→                                                           sensor:Cancelled )
   252→                                             ]
   253→                        ] .
   254→```
   255→
   256→**OpenAPI:**
   257→```yaml
   258→PatternA:
   259→  type: object
   260→  required:
   261→    - isCategorisedBy
   262→  properties:
   263→    isCategorisedBy:
   264→      type: array
   265→      minItems: 1
   266→      items:
   267→        type: string
   268→        enum: [Active, Cancelled]
   269→```
   270→
   271→> **Note:** This variation applies equally to Pattern B. The `owl:equivalentClass` wrapper can be used with any of the restriction types (someValuesFrom, allValuesFrom, qualified cardinality, etc.).
   272→
   273→---
   274→
   275→## Pattern B: Typed Individuals (Class Enumeration)
   276→
   277→### Discriminator
   278→
   279→The restriction structure is **identical to Pattern A**—a blank node containing `owl:oneOf`. The distinction is in the **individuals themselves**:
   280→
   281→| Pattern | Individual typing | Metadata |
   282→|---------|-------------------|----------|
   283→| A | `owl:NamedIndividual` only | Just `rdfs:label` |
   284→| B | `owl:NamedIndividual` + domain class | `codeValue`, `displayName`, `definition`, etc. |
   285→
   286→The restriction can be attached via either `rdfs:subClassOf` or `owl:equivalentClass` (see Pattern A, Variation A7).
   287→
   288→**ASG detection:**
   289→```sparql
   290→# After extracting individuals from owl:oneOf list:
   291→SELECT ?individual ?domainClass WHERE {
   292→    ?individual a ?domainClass .
   293→    FILTER(?domainClass != owl:NamedIndividual)
   294→}
   295→# Results? → Pattern B
   296→# No results? → Pattern A
   297→```
   298→
   299→### Individuals Definition
   300→
   301→```turtle
   302→sensor:_sensor_type_analog rdf:type owl:NamedIndividual ,
   303→                                    sensor:SensorClass ;
   304→    sensor:hasSuperCategory sensor:_sensor_type ;
   305→    sensor:name "analog" ;
   306→    sensor:codeValue "analog" ;
   307→    sensor:displayName "Analog" ;
   308→    skos:definition "Analog sensor type." ;
   309→    skos:prefLabel "analog" ;
   310→    www:apiLabel "SensorAPIV1.0.0" .
   311→
   312→sensor:_sensor_type_digital rdf:type owl:NamedIndividual ,

   323→### All Variations
   324→
   325→```turtle
   326→sensor:PatternB rdf:type owl:Class ;

   328→        # B1: Required array (1..*) via someValuesFrom
   329→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   330→          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
   331→          owl:someValuesFrom [ rdf:type owl:Class ;

   353→        # B4: Required single (1..1) via qualifiedCardinality
   354→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   355→          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
   356→          owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   357→          owl:onClass [ rdf:type owl:Class ;

   360→                      ]
   361→        ] ,
   362→        # B5: Optional single (0..1) via maxQualifiedCardinality
   363→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;

   365→          owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;

   521→### Pattern B Advantages Over Pattern A
   522→
   523→| Capability | Pattern A | Pattern B |
   524→|------------|-----------|-----------|
   525→| Enumerate valid values via SPARQL | ✗ (parse ontology) | ✓ (`?x a SensorClass`) |
   526→| Rich metadata per value | ✗ | ✓ |
   527→| `apiLabel` on individuals | ✗ | ✓ |
   528→| Hierarchical grouping via `hasSuperCategory` | ✗ | ✓ |

   530→---
   531→
   532→## Pattern C: Class-based Taxonomy (Extensible/Hierarchical)
   533→
   534→### Discriminator
   535→
   536→The restriction target is a **named class IRI** (not a blank node). The class:
   537→- Has **no `owl:oneOf`** (open, not closed)
   538→- Is **NOT `rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept`** (not SKOS)
   539→- Has **no outgoing object property restrictions** (it's a taxonomy, not a domain class)
   540→- **May have subclasses** forming a hierarchy
   541→
   542→**ASG detection (SPARQL):**
   543→```sparql
   544→# Step 1: Target is named class IRI (not blank node)
   545→?restriction (owl:someValuesFrom|owl:allValuesFrom|owl:onClass) ?target .
   546→FILTER(isIRI(?target))
   547→
   548→# Step 2: Target has apiLabel
   549→?target www:apiLabel ?label .
   550→
   551→# Step 3: Not SKOS
   552→FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?target rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept }
   553→
   554→# Step 4: No owl:oneOf
   555→FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?target owl:oneOf ?list }
   556→FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?target owl:equivalentClass/owl:oneOf ?list }
   557→
   558→# Step 5: No outgoing object property restrictions (taxonomy, not domain class)
   559→FILTER NOT EXISTS {
   560→    ?target rdfs:subClassOf ?restriction2 .
   561→    ?restriction2 owl:onProperty ?prop .
   562→    ?prop a owl:ObjectProperty .
   563→    # Exclude properties to datatypes or to taxonomy classes themselves
   564→}
   565→```
   566→

   685→### All Variations
   686→
   687→```turtle
   688→sensor:PatternC rdf:type owl:Class ;
   689→    owl:equivalentClass
   690→        # C1: Required single (1..1) via equivalentClass
   691→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   692→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasEnvironment ;
   693→          owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   694→          owl:onClass sensor:EnvironmentCode
   695→        ] ;
   696→    rdfs:subClassOf
   697→        # C2: Required array (1..*) via someValuesFrom
   698→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   699→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasEnvironment ;
   700→          owl:someValuesFrom sensor:EnvironmentCode
   701→        ] ,
   702→        # C3: Type constraint (0..*) via allValuesFrom
   703→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   704→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasEnvironment ;
   705→          owl:allValuesFrom sensor:EnvironmentCode
   706→        ] ,
   707→        # C4: Required array (1..*) via minQualifiedCardinality
   708→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   709→          owl:onProperty sensor:isCategorisedBy ;
   710→          owl:minQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   711→          owl:onClass sensor:EnvironmentCode
   712→        ] ,
   713→        # C5: Required single (1..1) via qualifiedCardinality
   714→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   715→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasEnvironment ;
   716→          owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   717→          owl:onClass sensor:EnvironmentCode
   718→        ] ,
   719→        # C6: Bounded array (0..3) via maxQualifiedCardinality
   720→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   721→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasEnvironment ;
   722→          owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "3"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
   723→          owl:onClass sensor:EnvironmentCode
   724→        ] ,
   725→        # C7: Optional array (0..*) via allValuesFrom - flat taxonomy (no subclasses)
   726→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
   727→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasTag ;
   728→          owl:allValuesFrom sensor:TagCode
   729→        ] ;
   730→    skos:definition "Class-Based Restrictions. Best for: Extensible categories" ;
   731→    skos:prefLabel "Pattern C" .
   732→```

   912→
   913→### Pattern B vs C Comparison
   914→
   915→| Aspect | Pattern B | Pattern C |
   916→|--------|-----------|-----------|
   917→| Restriction target | Blank node with `owl:oneOf` | Named class IRI |
   918→| Set closure | Closed (explicit list) | Open (query instances) |
   919→| Class structure | Flat (one class) | Flat OR hierarchical (subclasses) |
   920→| Adding values | Requires changing `owl:oneOf` | Just add instance |
   921→| Discovery | Parse `owl:oneOf` | Query `?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* <Class>` |
   922→| OpenAPI output | Inline enum or `$ref` to enum | `$ref` to extensible schema |
   923→
   924→### Pattern C Variants
   925→
   926→| Variant | Example | Class Structure | OpenAPI |
   927→|---------|---------|-----------------|---------|
   928→| Hierarchical | `EnvironmentCode` | Has subclasses (`IndoorEnvironment`, etc.) | Schema with `x-hierarchy` |
   929→| Flat | `TagCode` | Direct instances only | Schema without `x-hierarchy` |
   930→
   931→### Pattern C Advantages
   932→
   933→| Capability | Pattern A/B | Pattern C |
   934→|------------|-------------|-----------|
   935→| Add values without schema change | ✗ | ✓ |
   936→| Hierarchical grouping | ✗ | ✓ |
   937→| Query by category type | ✗ | ✓ (`?x a IndoorEnvironment`) |
   938→| Inheritance of properties | ✗ | ✓ (subclasses inherit) |
   939→
   940→---
   941→
   942→## Pattern D: SKOS Taxonomy (FHIR-Compatible)
   943→
   944→### Discriminator
   945→
   946→Restriction target is a class that:
   947→- Is `rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept`, OR
   948→- Has `owl:equivalentClass` with restriction on `skos:inScheme`
   949→
   950→**ASG detection (SPARQL):**
   951→```sparql
   952→# Check for SKOS subclass
   953→{ ?target rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept }
   954→UNION
   955→# Check for equivalentClass with inScheme restriction
   956→{ ?target owl:equivalentClass ?equiv .
   957→  ?equiv owl:onProperty skos:inScheme ;
   958→         owl:hasValue ?scheme . }
   959→```
   960→
   961→### FHIR Terminology Concepts
   962→
   963→| FHIR Resource | Purpose | OWL/SKOS Mapping |
   964→|---------------|---------|------------------|
   965→| **CodeSystem** | Defines codes and their meanings | `skos:ConceptScheme` |
   966→| **ValueSet** | Selects codes from CodeSystem(s) for use in a context | `skos:Collection` with `skos:member` |
   967→| **Coding** | Single code instance: system + code + display | `skos:Concept` with `skos:notation`, `skos:prefLabel` |
   968→| **CodeableConcept** | Multiple Codings representing same concept | Array of Coding objects |
   969→| **Binding** | Links element to ValueSet with strength | OWL restriction + `fhir:binding` annotation |
   970→
   971→### Full FHIR-Compatible Model
   972→
   973→#### 1. CodeSystem (defines the codes)
   974→
   975→```turtle
   976→sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem a skos:ConceptScheme ;
   977→    rdfs:label "Measurement Context Code System"@en ;
   978→    skos:definition "Code system defining measurement context types"@en ;
   979→    fhir:url "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/CodeSystem/measurement-context"^^xsd:anyURI ;
   980→    fhir:version "1.0.0" ;
   981→    fhir:status "active" ;
   982→    skos:hasTopConcept sensor:context_continuous,
   983→                       sensor:context_discrete .
   984→```
   985→
   986→#### 2. Concepts (defined by the CodeSystem)
   987→
   988→```turtle
   989→sensor:context_continuous a skos:Concept ;
   990→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
   991→    skos:topConceptOf sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
   992→    skos:notation "continuous" ;
   993→    skos:prefLabel "Continuous Measurement"@en ;
   994→    skos:definition "Ongoing continuous measurement"@en ;
   995→    skos:narrower sensor:context_realtime,
   996→                  sensor:context_sampled .
   997→
   998→sensor:context_realtime a skos:Concept ;
   999→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1000→    skos:notation "realtime" ;
  1001→    skos:prefLabel "Real-time"@en ;
  1002→    skos:altLabel "Live"@en ;
  1003→    skos:definition "Real-time continuous streaming"@en ;
  1004→    skos:broader sensor:context_continuous ;
  1005→    skos:narrower sensor:context_realtime_high_freq,
  1006→                  sensor:context_realtime_standard .
  1007→
  1008→sensor:context_realtime_high_freq a skos:Concept ;
  1009→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1010→    skos:notation "realtime-hf" ;
  1011→    skos:prefLabel "Real-time High Frequency"@en ;
  1012→    skos:definition "High-frequency real-time (>1000 samples/sec)"@en ;
  1013→    skos:broader sensor:context_realtime .
  1014→
  1015→sensor:context_realtime_standard a skos:Concept ;
  1016→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1017→    skos:notation "realtime-std" ;
  1018→    skos:prefLabel "Real-time Standard"@en ;
  1019→    skos:definition "Standard frequency real-time (1-1000 samples/sec)"@en ;
  1020→    skos:broader sensor:context_realtime .
  1021→
  1022→sensor:context_discrete a skos:Concept ;
  1023→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1024→    skos:topConceptOf sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1025→    skos:notation "discrete" ;
  1026→    skos:prefLabel "Discrete Measurement"@en ;
  1027→    skos:definition "Point-in-time discrete measurements"@en ;
  1028→    skos:narrower sensor:context_scheduled,
  1029→                  sensor:context_triggered .
  1030→
  1031→sensor:context_scheduled a skos:Concept ;
  1032→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1033→    skos:notation "scheduled" ;
  1034→    skos:prefLabel "Scheduled"@en ;
  1035→    skos:altLabel "Batch"@en ;
  1036→    skos:definition "Measurement at scheduled times"@en ;
  1037→    skos:broader sensor:context_discrete .
  1038→
  1039→sensor:context_triggered a skos:Concept ;
  1040→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1041→    skos:notation "triggered" ;
  1042→    skos:prefLabel "Event Triggered"@en ;
  1043→    skos:altLabel "On-Demand"@en ;
  1044→    skos:definition "Measurement triggered by external event"@en ;
  1045→    skos:broader sensor:context_discrete ;
  1046→    skos:narrower sensor:context_triggered_alarm,
  1047→                  sensor:context_triggered_threshold .
  1048→```
  1049→
  1050→#### 3. ValueSet (selects codes for a specific use)
  1051→
  1052→**Full CodeSystem inclusion:**
  1053→```turtle
  1054→sensor:AllMeasurementContextsValueSet a skos:Collection ;
  1055→    rdfs:label "All Measurement Contexts Value Set"@en ;
  1056→    skos:definition "Value set including all measurement context codes"@en ;
  1057→    fhir:url "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/all-measurement-contexts"^^xsd:anyURI ;
  1058→    fhir:version "1.0.0" ;
  1059→    fhir:status "active" .
  1060→    # Membership defined via bridge class equivalentClass
  1061→```
  1062→
  1063→**Subset ValueSet:**
  1064→```turtle
  1065→sensor:RealtimeMeasurementValueSet a skos:Collection ;
  1066→    rdfs:label "Realtime Measurement Value Set"@en ;
  1067→    skos:definition "Value set for real-time measurement contexts only"@en ;
  1068→    fhir:url "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/realtime-measurement"^^xsd:anyURI ;
  1069→    fhir:version "1.0.0" ;
  1070→    fhir:status "active" ;
  1071→    skos:member sensor:context_realtime,
  1072→                sensor:context_realtime_high_freq,
  1073→                sensor:context_realtime_standard .
  1074→```
  1075→
  1076→#### 4. Bridge Class (makes ValueSet usable in OWL restrictions)
  1077→
  1078→**Bridge for all contexts (full CodeSystem):**
  1079→```turtle
  1080→sensor:MeasurementContextCode a owl:Class ;
  1081→    rdfs:label "Measurement Context Code"@en ;
  1082→    rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept ;
  1083→    owl:equivalentClass [
  1084→        a owl:Restriction ;
  1085→        owl:onProperty skos:inScheme ;
  1086→        owl:hasValue sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem
  1087→    ] ;
  1088→    fhir:binding [
  1089→        fhir:valueSet "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/all-measurement-contexts"^^xsd:anyURI ;
  1090→        fhir:strength "required"
  1091→    ] .
  1092→```
  1093→
  1094→**Bridge for subset (Realtime only):**
  1095→```turtle
  1096→sensor:RealtimeMeasurementCode a owl:Class ;
  1097→    rdfs:label "Realtime Measurement Code"@en ;
  1098→    rdfs:subClassOf sensor:MeasurementContextCode ;
  1099→    owl:equivalentClass [
  1100→        a owl:Class ;
  1101→        owl:intersectionOf (
  1102→            skos:Concept
  1103→            [ a owl:Restriction ;
  1104→              owl:onProperty skos:inScheme ;
  1105→              owl:hasValue sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ]
  1106→            [ a owl:Restriction ;
  1107→              owl:onProperty skos:broader ;
  1108→              owl:someValuesFrom [ owl:oneOf ( sensor:context_realtime ) ] ]
  1109→        )
  1110→    ] ;
  1111→    fhir:binding [
  1112→        fhir:valueSet "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/realtime-measurement"^^xsd:anyURI ;
  1113→        fhir:strength "required"
  1114→    ] .
  1115→```
  1116→
  1117→#### 5. Binding (restriction on domain class)
  1118→
  1119→```turtle
  1120→sensor:Sensor rdfs:subClassOf [
  1121→    a owl:Restriction ;
  1122→    owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
  1123→    owl:someValuesFrom sensor:MeasurementContextCode  # All contexts allowed
  1124→] .
  1125→
  1126→sensor:RealtimeSensor rdfs:subClassOf
  1127→    sensor:Sensor ,
  1128→    [ a owl:Restriction ;
  1129→      owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
  1130→      owl:someValuesFrom sensor:RealtimeMeasurementCode  # Only realtime subset
  1131→    ] .
  1132→```
  1133→
  1134→### All Variations
  1135→
  1136→```turtle
  1137→sensor:PatternD rdf:type owl:Class ;
  1138→    rdfs:subClassOf
  1139→        # D1: Required array (1..*) via someValuesFrom
  1140→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
  1141→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
  1142→          owl:someValuesFrom sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1143→        ] ,
  1144→        # D2: Type constraint (0..*) via allValuesFrom
  1145→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
  1146→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
  1147→          owl:allValuesFrom sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1148→        ] ,
  1149→        # D3: Required single (1..1) via qualifiedCardinality
  1150→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
  1151→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
  1152→          owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
  1153→          owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1154→        ] ,
  1155→        # D4: Optional single (0..1) via maxQualifiedCardinality
  1156→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
  1157→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
  1158→          owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
  1159→          owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1160→        ] ,
  1161→        # D5: Bounded array (1..3) via min + max
  1162→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
  1163→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
  1164→          owl:minQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
  1165→          owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1166→        ] ,
  1167→        [ rdf:type owl:Restriction ;
  1168→          owl:onProperty sensor:hasMeasurementContext ;
  1169→          owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "3"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
  1170→          owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1171→        ] ;
  1172→    skos:definition "SKOS Taxonomy. Best for: Standards-compliant vocabularies" ;
  1173→    skos:prefLabel "Pattern D" .
  1174→```
  1175→
  1176→### Variation D1: Required Array (1..*) via someValuesFrom
  1177→
  1178→**OWL:**
  1179→```turtle
  1180→owl:someValuesFrom sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1181→```
  1182→
  1183→**OpenAPI (Coding style):**
  1184→```yaml
  1185→PatternD:
  1186→  type: object
  1187→  required:
  1188→    - hasMeasurementContext
  1189→  properties:
  1190→    hasMeasurementContext:
  1191→      type: array
  1192→      minItems: 1
  1193→      items:
  1194→        $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
  1195→```
  1196→
  1197→### Variation D2: Type Constraint / Optional Array (0..*) via allValuesFrom
  1198→
  1199→**OWL:**
  1200→```turtle
  1201→owl:allValuesFrom sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1202→```
  1203→
  1204→**OpenAPI:**
  1205→```yaml
  1206→PatternD:
  1207→  type: object
  1208→  properties:
  1209→    hasMeasurementContext:
  1210→      type: array
  1211→      items:
  1212→        $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
  1213→```
  1214→
  1215→### Variation D3: Required Single (1..1) via qualifiedCardinality
  1216→
  1217→**OWL:**
  1218→```turtle
  1219→owl:qualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
  1220→owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1221→```
  1222→
  1223→**OpenAPI:**
  1224→```yaml
  1225→PatternD:
  1226→  type: object
  1227→  required:
  1228→    - hasMeasurementContext
  1229→  properties:
  1230→    hasMeasurementContext:
  1231→      $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
  1232→```
  1233→
  1234→### Variation D4: Optional Single (0..1) via maxQualifiedCardinality
  1235→
  1236→**OWL:**
  1237→```turtle
  1238→owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
  1239→owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1240→```
  1241→
  1242→**OpenAPI:**
  1243→```yaml
  1244→PatternD:
  1245→  type: object
  1246→  properties:
  1247→    hasMeasurementContext:
  1248→      $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
  1249→```
  1250→
  1251→### Variation D5: Bounded Array (1..3) via min + max
  1252→
  1253→**OWL:**
  1254→```turtle
  1255→owl:minQualifiedCardinality "1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
  1256→owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1257→# Combined with:
  1258→owl:maxQualifiedCardinality "3"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger ;
  1259→owl:onClass sensor:MeasurementContextCode
  1260→```
  1261→
  1262→**OpenAPI:**
  1263→```yaml
  1264→PatternD:
  1265→  type: object
  1266→  required:
  1267→    - hasMeasurementContext
  1268→  properties:
  1269→    hasMeasurementContext:
  1270→      type: array
  1271→      minItems: 1
  1272→      maxItems: 3
  1273→      items:
  1274→        $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
  1275→```
  1276→
  1277→### OpenAPI Schemas for FHIR-Compatible Output
  1278→
  1279→**Coding (single code):**
  1280→```yaml
  1281→components:
  1282→  schemas:
  1283→    MeasurementContextCoding:
  1284→      type: object
  1285→      required:
  1286→        - code
  1287→      properties:
  1288→        system:
  1289→          type: string
  1290→          const: "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/CodeSystem/measurement-context"
  1291→          description: The CodeSystem URL
  1292→        code:
  1293→          type: string
  1294→          description: The code value (skos:notation)
  1295→        display:
  1296→          type: string
  1297→          description: Human-readable display (skos:prefLabel)
  1298→      x-fhir-valueset: "https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/ValueSet/all-measurement-contexts"
  1299→      x-fhir-binding-strength: required
  1300→      x-skos-hierarchy:
  1301→        continuous:
  1302→          children: [realtime, sampled]
  1303→        realtime:
  1304→          parent: continuous
  1305→          children: [realtime-hf, realtime-std]
  1306→        realtime-hf:
  1307→          parent: realtime
  1308→        realtime-std:
  1309→          parent: realtime
  1310→        sampled:
  1311→          parent: continuous
  1312→        discrete:
  1313→          children: [scheduled, triggered]
  1314→        scheduled:
  1315→          parent: discrete
  1316→        triggered:
  1317→          parent: discrete
  1318→          children: [triggered-alarm, triggered-threshold]
  1319→```
  1320→
  1321→**CodeableConcept (multiple codings):**
  1322→```yaml
  1323→    MeasurementContextCodeableConcept:
  1324→      type: object
  1325→      properties:
  1326→        coding:
  1327→          type: array
  1328→          items:
  1329→            $ref: '#/components/schemas/MeasurementContextCoding'
  1330→          description: Code(s) from controlled vocabularies
  1331→        text:
  1332→          type: string
  1333→          description: Plain text representation chosen by user
  1334→```
  1335→
  1336→### Pattern D Discovery (ASG)
  1337→
  1338→```sparql
  1339→# Find all concepts in a scheme
  1340→SELECT ?concept ?code ?display ?broader WHERE {
  1341→    ?concept skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1342→             skos:notation ?code ;
  1343→             skos:prefLabel ?display .
  1344→    OPTIONAL { ?concept skos:broader ?broader }
  1345→}
  1346→ORDER BY ?broader ?code
  1347→```
  1348→
  1349→### FHIR Binding Strengths
  1350→
  1351→| Strength | Meaning | OWL Pattern |
  1352→|----------|---------|-------------|
  1353→| **required** | Must be from ValueSet | `owl:someValuesFrom` + `owl:allValuesFrom` |
  1354→| **extensible** | Should be from ValueSet, can extend | `owl:someValuesFrom` only |
  1355→| **preferred** | Encouraged but not required | `owl:someValuesFrom` with broader class |
  1356→| **example** | Illustrative only | `rdfs:range` only (no restriction) |
  1357→
  1358→### Pattern C vs D Comparison
  1359→
  1360→| Aspect | Pattern C (Class-based) | Pattern D (SKOS) |
  1361→|--------|------------------------|------------------|
  1362→| Standard | OWL only | OWL + SKOS + FHIR |
  1363→| Hierarchy | `rdfs:subClassOf` | `skos:broader`/`skos:narrower` |
  1364→| Membership | `rdf:type` | `skos:inScheme` |
  1365→| Subset selection | Subclass hierarchy | `skos:Collection` + `skos:member` |
  1366→| Multi-language | Not native | `skos:prefLabel`/`skos:altLabel` with `@lang` |
  1367→| External mapping | Not native | `skos:exactMatch`, `skos:closeMatch` |
  1368→| Interoperability | OWL tools only | FHIR, SKOS, terminology servers |
  1369→| Discovery | `?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* Class` | `?x skos:inScheme Scheme` |
  1370→
  1371→### Pattern D Advantages
  1372→
  1373→| Capability | Pattern A/B/C | Pattern D |
  1374→|------------|---------------|-----------|
  1375→| FHIR compatibility | ✗ | ✓ |
  1376→| Terminology server support | ✗ | ✓ |
  1377→| Multi-language labels | ✗ | ✓ |
  1378→| Alternative labels | ✗ | ✓ (`skos:altLabel`) |
  1379→| External code mapping | ✗ | ✓ (`skos:exactMatch`) |
  1380→| Transitive hierarchy queries | Limited | ✓ (`skos:broader+`) |
  1381→| ValueSet subsetting | ✗ | ✓ |
  1382→| Binding strength | ✗ | ✓ |
  1383→
  1384→### Comparison: Pattern D vs FHIR RDF Ontology
  1385→
  1386→The official FHIR RDF specification takes a different approach to terminology than our SKOS-based Pattern D. Here's how they compare:
  1387→
  1388→#### FHIR RDF Ontology Approach
  1389→
  1390→FHIR RDF represents coded values as **structured objects** with properties, not as direct concept references:
  1391→
  1392→```turtle
  1393→# FHIR RDF Coding structure
  1394→fhir:CodingBase.system rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .
  1395→fhir:CodingBase.code rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .
  1396→fhir:CodingBase.display rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .
  1397→fhir:CodingBase.version rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .
  1398→
  1399→# Example instance (FHIR RDF style)
  1400→:observation1 fhir:Observation.status [
  1401→    fhir:Coding.system [ fhir:value "http://hl7.org/fhir/observation-status"^^xsd:anyURI ] ;
  1402→    fhir:Coding.code [ fhir:value "final" ] ;
  1403→    fhir:Coding.display [ fhir:value "Final" ]
  1404→] .
  1405→```
  1406→
  1407→#### Pattern D (SKOS) Approach
  1408→
  1409→Pattern D uses **direct concept references** with SKOS vocabulary:
  1410→
  1411→```turtle
  1412→# Pattern D structure
  1413→sensor:context_realtime a skos:Concept ;
  1414→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1415→    skos:notation "realtime" ;
  1416→    skos:prefLabel "Real-time"@en .
  1417→
  1418→# Example instance (Pattern D style)
  1419→:sensor1 sensor:hasMeasurementContext sensor:context_realtime .
  1420→```
  1421→
  1422→#### Key Differences
  1423→
  1424→| Aspect | FHIR RDF Ontology | Pattern D (SKOS) |
  1425→|--------|-------------------|------------------|
  1426→| **Code representation** | Anonymous node with properties | Named individual (IRI) |
  1427→| **Value access** | `fhir:Coding.code/fhir:value` | Direct object property |
  1428→| **System identification** | Explicit `fhir:Coding.system` property | Implicit via `skos:inScheme` |
  1429→| **Display text** | Explicit `fhir:Coding.display` property | `skos:prefLabel` on concept |
  1430→| **Hierarchy** | Not in Coding structure | `skos:broader/narrower` on concepts |
  1431→| **Round-tripping** | Optimized for JSON/XML ↔ RDF | Native RDF/OWL semantics |
  1432→| **Reasoning** | Limited (data-oriented) | Full OWL DL reasoning |
  1433→| **External ontologies** | Optional `rdf:type` extension | `skos:exactMatch`, `skos:closeMatch` |
  1434→
  1435→#### FHIR RDF Extensions for Ontology Integration
  1436→
  1437→FHIR RDF provides optional extensions to bridge to external ontologies:
  1438→
  1439→```json
  1440→{
  1441→  "code": {
  1442→    "extension": {
  1443→      "url": "http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/rdftype",
  1444→      "valueUri": "http://snomed.info/id/24484000"
  1445→    },
  1446→    "coding": {
  1447→      "system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
  1448→      "code": "24484000",
  1449→      "display": "Severe"
  1450→    }
  1451→  }
  1452→}
  1453→```
  1454→
  1455→This allows FHIR instances to carry an `rdf:type` assertion linking to external ontology concepts, while maintaining JSON/XML compatibility.
  1456→
  1457→#### When to Use Each Approach
  1458→
  1459→| Use Case | Recommended Approach |
  1460→|----------|---------------------|
  1461→| Exchanging FHIR resources | FHIR RDF (for round-tripping) |
  1462→| Internal ontology modeling | Pattern D (SKOS) |
  1463→| Terminology server integration | Either (both support FHIR API) |
  1464→| OWL reasoning over codes | Pattern D (SKOS) |
  1465→| Multi-system code mapping | Pattern D (via `skos:exactMatch`) |
  1466→| Generating APIs from ontology | Pattern D (cleaner mapping) |
  1467→
  1468→#### Hybrid Approach for FHIR Interoperability
  1469→
  1470→For maximum interoperability, you can support both:
  1471→
  1472→```turtle
  1473→# Define concepts in SKOS (Pattern D)
  1474→sensor:context_realtime a skos:Concept ;
  1475→    skos:inScheme sensor:MeasurementContextCodeSystem ;
  1476→    skos:notation "realtime" ;
  1477→    skos:prefLabel "Real-time"@en ;
  1478→    # Map to FHIR coding
  1479→    fhir:system "https://graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/CodeSystem/measurement-context"^^xsd:anyURI ;
  1480→    fhir:code "realtime" ;
  1481→    fhir:display "Real-time" .
  1482→
  1483→# In instance data, use direct reference (Pattern D style)
  1484→:sensor1 sensor:hasMeasurementContext sensor:context_realtime .
  1485→
  1486→# For FHIR export, generate Coding structure
  1487→# {
  1488→#   "system": "https://graphresearchlabs.com/sensor/CodeSystem/measurement-context",
  1489→#   "code": "realtime",
  1490→#   "display": "Real-time"
  1491→# }
  1492→```
  1493→
  1494→This allows:
  1495→1. **Native OWL reasoning** via direct concept references
  1496→2. **FHIR JSON/XML export** via properties on concepts
  1497→3. **Terminology server queries** via SKOS structure
  1498→
  1499→---
  1500→
  1501→## ASG Decision Tree
  1502→
  1503→```
  1504→1. EXTRACT RESTRICTION from class definition
  1505→   Check both rdfs:subClassOf and owl:equivalentClass
  1506→
  1507→2. EXTRACT TARGET from restriction
  1508→   ├─ someValuesFrom → target
  1509→   ├─ allValuesFrom → target
  1510→   └─ onClass → target
  1511→
  1512→3. IS TARGET A BLANK NODE?
  1513→   └─ YES → Check for owl:oneOf inside blank node
  1514→            └─ Found owl:oneOf? Extract individuals from list
  1515→               └─ Do individuals have a domain class type (not just owl:NamedIndividual)?
  1516→                  ├─ YES → Pattern B (typed individuals)
  1517→                  │        Parse oneOf, emit enum with x-code-metadata
  1518→                  └─ NO  → Pattern A (inline enum)
  1519→                           Parse oneOf, emit simple inline enum
  1520→   └─ NO (target is named class IRI) → Continue to step 4
  1521→
  1522→4. DOES TARGET HAVE apiLabel MATCHING CURRENT API SUFFIX?
  1523→   └─ NO  → Stop traversal (not included in this API)
  1524→   └─ YES → Continue to step 5
  1525→
  1526→5. IS TARGET rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept?
  1527→   OR HAS owl:equivalentClass WITH skos:inScheme RESTRICTION?
  1528→   └─ YES → Pattern D (SKOS)
  1529→            Query: ?x skos:inScheme <scheme>
  1530→            Emit: Coding schema with hierarchy
  1531→   └─ NO  → Continue to step 6
  1532→
  1533→6. DOES TARGET HAVE owl:oneOf (direct or via equivalentClass)?
  1534→   └─ YES → Pattern B variant (named class enum)
  1535→            Parse oneOf list, emit enum schema with x-code-metadata
  1536→   └─ NO  → Continue to step 7
  1537→
  1538→7. DOES TARGET HAVE OUTGOING OBJECT PROPERTY RESTRICTIONS?
  1539→   (i.e., rdfs:subClassOf [ owl:onProperty ?p ] where ?p is owl:ObjectProperty
  1540→    pointing to another domain class)
  1541→   └─ YES → Domain class, not taxonomy
  1542→            Continue property traversal into this class
  1543→   └─ NO  → Pattern C (class-based taxonomy)
  1544→            Query: ?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* <TargetClass>
  1545→            Emit: $ref to extensible schema with x-hierarchy
  1546→```
  1547→
  1548→---
  1549→
  1550→## Cardinality Summary
  1551→
  1552→| OWL Pattern | Cardinality | Required? | Array? |
  1553→|-------------|-------------|-----------|--------|
  1554→| `owl:qualifiedCardinality 1` | 1..1 | Yes | No |
  1555→| `owl:maxQualifiedCardinality 1` | 0..1 | No | No |
  1556→| `owl:someValuesFrom` | 1..* | Yes | Yes |
  1557→| `owl:allValuesFrom` (alone) | 0..* | No | Yes |
  1558→| `owl:someValuesFrom` + `owl:allValuesFrom` | 1..* (constrained) | Yes | Yes |
  1559→| `owl:minQualifiedCardinality n` | n..* | Yes (if n≥1) | Yes |
  1560→| `owl:maxQualifiedCardinality n` | 0..n | No | Yes (bounded) |
  1561→
  1562→---
  1563→
  1564→## Annotation Requirements
  1565→
  1566→| Pattern | What needs `apiLabel` matching API suffix |
  1567→|---------|------------------------------------------|
  1568→| A | Property only (inline `owl:oneOf` parsed, individuals have no class) |
  1569→| B | Property + individuals (individuals have `apiLabel` for API inclusion) |
  1570→| C | Property + taxonomy class + subclasses (if hierarchical) |
  1571→| D | Property + bridge class (concepts discovered via `skos:inScheme`) |
  1572→
  1573→**Pattern A vs B annotation difference:**
  1574→- Pattern A: Individuals discovered from `owl:oneOf`, no `apiLabel` needed on individuals
  1575→- Pattern B: Individuals have domain class and `apiLabel` for multi-API support
  1576→
  1577→**Pattern C annotation notes:**
  1578→- The taxonomy class (`EnvironmentCode`) needs `apiLabel`
  1579→- Subclasses (`IndoorEnvironment`, `IndustrialEnvironment`, `OutdoorEnvironment`) need `apiLabel` if they should appear in the API hierarchy
  1580→- Individuals are discovered via query, so they don't need `apiLabel` unless filtered by API
  1581→
  1582→**Pattern D annotation notes:**
  1583→- The bridge class (`MeasurementContextCode`) needs `apiLabel`
  1584→- The `skos:ConceptScheme` does NOT need `apiLabel` (it's not an OWL class)
  1585→- `skos:Concept` instances do NOT need `apiLabel` — discovered via `skos:inScheme` query
  1586→- ValueSet collections do NOT need `apiLabel` — referenced via bridge class binding
  1587→
